claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 12 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for.**

**How old is the child you want to claim Child Benefit for?**

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children, let's start with one child first, and we can check the others afterwards. Please also let me know the child's name so I can keep track.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes, I'm looking to claim for my son Alex. He's 12 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information! Since Alex is 12 years old (under 16), he automatically meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about Alex's work status.

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Since Alex is only 12, I assume he's not working - but please confirm this is the case.

- **Yes** - Alex works less than 24 hours per week or doesn't work at all
- **No** - Alex works 24 hours or more per week

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, he doesn't work at all. He's still in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for confirming. Since Alex doesn't work at all, he meets this requirement. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good progress! Now I need to check about benefits.

**Is Alex NOT receiving any of the following benefits in his own name?**
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

In other words, is Alex free from receiving these benefits himself? (This is almost always "yes" for a 12-year-old, but I need to confirm.)

- **Yes** - Alex is NOT receiving ESA or Universal Credit in his own right
- **No** - Alex IS receiving one of these benefits

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming. Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name, so we can move on to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about apprenticeships.

**Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?**

Since you mentioned he's still in school at 12 years old, I expect the answer is no - but please confirm:

- **No, Alex is not in an apprenticeship** (most likely for a 12-year-old)
- **Yes, Alex is in an apprenticeship** (if so, I'd need to know which type and region)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship. He's just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected. Since Alex is not in an apprenticeship, let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status. 

**Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements?**

You only need to meet **ONE** of these to qualify:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You are a regular UK resident
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (not claiming certain benefits)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status, are studying, and have sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as a family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

**Do any of these apply to you?** The most common answer is simply that you normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I normally live in the UK. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you! Since you normally live in the UK, you meet the residency requirement. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your responsibility for Alex.

**Are you responsible for Alex in any of the following ways?**

You only need to meet **ONE** of these:

1. **Living with Alex** - Alex lives with you
2. **Contributing to Alex's upkeep** - You pay at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards his care (this includes food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents, etc.)
3. **Paying maintenance** - You make maintenance payments under a court order or agreement that cover Alex's upkeep

**Which of these applies to you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Since Alex lives with you, you meet the responsibility requirement. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good progress! Now I need to check about other potential claimants.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

- **No** - No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- **Yes, but they've agreed to transfer the claim to me** - Someone else is claiming but you've agreed that you should receive it instead
- **Yes, and there's a dispute** - Someone else is claiming and you cannot agree on who should receive it

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that keeps things straightforward. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check Alex's current living situation.

**Where is Alex currently living?**

- **At home/normal living situation** - Alex lives at home with you under normal circumstances
- **In hospital** - Alex is currently staying in hospital
- **In local authority care** - Alex is in the care of a local authority
- **In a foster arrangement** - Alex is being fostered

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's at home with me, just normal living.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Alex is living at home with you under normal circumstances. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
We're almost there! Just one final check.

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, are you supporting Alex without financial assistance from the local council for his accommodation or upkeep?

- **Yes** - The local council is NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance (most common)
- **No** - The local council IS paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - the council isn't paying anything towards his accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! That was the final check. Let me navigate to the outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great news! Based on your answers, I can now provide my assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Good News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit for Alex

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for your son Alex.

### Summary of Why You're Eligible:
✅ Alex is under 16 years old (12 years old)  
✅ Alex is not working  
✅ Alex doesn't receive benefits in his own name  
✅ Alex is not in an apprenticeship (he's in regular school)  
✅ You normally live in the UK  
✅ You are responsible for Alex (he lives with you)  
✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex  
✅ The local council isn't paying towards Alex's care  

---

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** (or adoption certificate if applicable)

### Important Information:
- **Payments** are made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)
- ⚠️ **High Income Child Benefit Charge**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may need to pay some or all of it back through a tax charge. You can still claim, but you may choose to opt out of payments to avoid the charge.

### Useful Link:
🔗 [How to Claim Child Benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

---

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit, or do you have any other children you'd like me to check eligibility for?

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) He is under 16 years old (12 years old), which automatically qualifies him for the age requirement; (2) He is not working (under 24 hours per week requirement met); (3) He does not receive any benefits in his own name (ESA or Universal Credit); (4) He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship - he's in regular school; (5) His parent normally lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements; (6) His parent is responsible for him as Alex lives at home with them; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex is living at home under normal circumstances; (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. All eligibility criteria have been met: Alex is a qualifying child (under 16, in school, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship), the claimant meets residency requirements (normally lives in the UK), they are responsible for Alex (he lives with them), there are no competing claimants, and no council payments are being made towards Alex's care.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit

Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) He is under 16 years old (12 years old), which automatically qualifies him for the age requirement; (2) He is not working (under 24 hours per week requirement met); (3) He does not receive any benefits in his own name (ESA or Universal Credit); (4) He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship - he's in regular school; (5) His parent normally lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements; (6) His parent is responsible for him as Alex lives at home with them; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex is living at home under normal circumstances; (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. All eligibility criteria have been met: Alex is a qualifying child (under 16, in school, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship), the claimant meets residency requirements (normally lives in the UK), they are responsible for Alex (he lives with them), there are no competing claimants, and no council payments are being made towards Alex's care.
